data.title: Graphic Packaging downgraded by RBC on inflation, oversupply, cost overruns
data.description:
Graphic Packaging Holding Company (NYSE:GPK) late Wednesday was downgraded to Sector Perform from Outperform by RBC Capital Markets, with analysts saying that ongoing cost pressures, weak consumer packaged goods volumes and bleached paperboard oversupply weigh on earnings potential.

Volume weakness, inflationary pressures

Analyst Arun Viswanathan wrote that the company’s core food packaging volumes have softened, with July flat but August declining 2% and continued weakness likely into September. Inflation is pressuring consumers and limiting promotional activity, while reformulation costs tied to “Make America Healthy Again” initiatives could further strain brand spending.

RBC cut its 2025 and 2026 earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization forecasts to $1.435 billion and $1.5 billion, respectively, down from $1.485 billion and $1.55 billion.

Bleached paperboard oversupply compresses margins

A major concern for margins is oversupply in the bleached paperboard market. Sappi’s recent 520,000-ton capacity addition has narrowed the premium over recycled grades to just 7%, compared with a historical range of 40–60%.

This dynamic, RBC said, has limited Graphic Packaging’s (NYSE:GPK) ability to raise prices across coated recycled and unbleached board, which represent roughly 80% of sales. As a result, ebitda margins are projected at just 17% to 18% through 2026.

Waco project cost overruns

Graphic Packaging (GPK) also raised its 2025 capital spending guidance to $850 million, up from $700 million, due to cost overruns at its Waco, Texas project. RBC estimates the higher outlay could extend the company’s deleveraging timeline and limit shareholder returns. “Each 0.5x of leverage or EBITDA is worth about $3 per share to our price target,” the analysts wrote.

Valuation and outlook

At $18.79, GPK shares trade at roughly 9.4 times RBC’s 2026 adjusted earnings estimate of $2.00 a share. The new $21 price target, down from $25 previously, reflects a 7.5x multiple on estimated 2026 ebitda, in line with packaging peers. The analysts set an upside scenario of $32, assuming stronger productivity gains and higher multiples, and a downside of $14 if volumes and pricing remain weak.

While RBC acknowledged Graphic Packaging’s (GPK) strong capital allocation record and long-term opportunity to benefit from consumer demand for sustainable packaging, the firm said it is waiting for “lower inflation, more promotion, and stronger volumes” before becoming more constructive.

data.title: Wendy's launches 'Project Fresh' initiative to address sluggish growth, boost valuation
data.description:
To raise its valuation and put the company “on the right path to increase value for franchisees, employees, and shareholders,” Wendy’s (NASDAQ:WEN) is initiating “Project Fresh” to revitalize the Wendy’s (NASDAQ:WEN) brand, reallocate capital, and optimize operations.

"Wendy's Board of Directors and management team are dissatisfied with the current valuation of the Company, said Wendy’s chairman Art Winkleblack. “Execution is underway and we are confident that Project Fresh will position Wendy’s as the industry leader.”

"With Project Fresh, we're taking decisive steps together as One Wendy's to strengthen our foundation, enhance restaurant performance and modernize how our customers experience the brand,” interim CEO and CFO Ken Cook added.

As part of the initiative, Wendy’s (NASDAQ:WEN) will engage Creed UnCo to create more effective marketing, optimize restaurant performance and maximize profitability with its franchisees, increase investments in technology to improve operations, and allocate capital more efficiently, including the reduction of capital allocated to the Build to Suit program by ~$20M and a larger anticipated reduction in 2026. This will allow Wendy’s to invest more in areas to drive average unit volume (AUV) growth.

The Build to Suit program was launched in May 2024 and designed to encourage the expansion of its franchises by minimizing the capital investment and offering franchisees support for site selection and construction. The company initially allocated $100M for the fund.

In its latest quarterly report, Wendy’s (WEN) lowered its full year guidance to reflect the lackluster economic environment in the U.S. and hinted at a plan to boost growth in the U.S., improve its marketing, and strengthen relationships with franchisees. Subsequently, the company’s shares saw a brief and shallow bounce, before resuming the drift lower. The stock bottomed out at a 5 1/2-year low on Thursday, losing nearly 50% in value from last year.

WEN is down another 0.9% on Thursday, the fourth straight day in the red.

data.title: AZZ shares tumble after quarterly earnings miss estimates
data.description:
AZZ (NYSE:AZZ) shares slumped as much as 13% on Thursday, a day after the company posted fiscal second-quarter results that fell short of Wall Street profit expectations.

The Texas-based provider of galvanizing and coil coating services reported adjusted earnings of $1.55 per share, up 13% from a year earlier but shy of analysts’ consensus estimate of $1.57. Revenue came in at $417.3 million, a 2% increase that missed the $426.16 million forecast.

Mixed segment performance

Metal Coatings, AZZ’s (NYSE:AZZ) galvanizing division, was a bright spot, generating $190 million in sales, a 10.8% jump on the strength of infrastructure-related demand in construction, industrial, and power transmission markets. The segment delivered an adjusted earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization margin of 30.8%, though margins dipped 90 basis points year-over-year due to a higher mix of large-scale electrical and solar projects.

In contrast, the Precoat Metals business struggled with weaker end-market demand in building construction, HVAC and appliances, with sales slipping 4.3% to $227.3 million. Segment ebitda fell to $45.9 million, with margins also narrowing by 90 basis points to 20.2%.

Profits, cash flow and M&A

On a GAAP basis, net income surged 152% to $89.3 million, or $2.95 a share, thanks in part to a distribution from its AVAIL joint venture. Adjusted net income totaled $46.9 million, up 14%. Operating cash flow rose 23% year-over-year to $58.4 million.

The company also completed the $30.1 million acquisition of a galvanizing facility in Canton, Ohio during the quarter, while returning $11.1 million to shareholders through dividends. Net leverage stood at 1.7 times trailing ebitda.

Guidance maintained

Despite the profit miss, AZZ (AZZ) reaffirmed its full-year 2026 guidance, projecting sales of $1.63 billion to $1.73 billion, adjusted ebitda of $360 million to $400 million, and adjusted earnings of $5.75 to $6.25 a share. Management pointed to a strong M&A pipeline, disciplined cost control and ongoing demand from infrastructure spending as reasons for confidence.

“Our pipeline of M&A opportunities remains robust, reflecting the strength of our strategy and our disciplined approach to pursuing high-quality acquisition targets,” Chief Executive Tom Ferguson said in a statement. “We continue to have confidence that our full-year 2026 financial guidance is achievable.”

data.title: Affirm, Klarna stocks surge on new collaboration with Google
data.description:
Affirm (NASDAQ:AFRM) and Klarna (NYSE:KLAR) stocks were trading higher after the payment processors announced collaboration with Alphabet's (NASDAQ:GOOG) (NASDAQ:GOOGL) (TSX:GOOG:CA) Google.

KLAR was +3.89% Thursday morning to $43.75, while AFRM was 5.12% up to $79.10.

Klarna said the strategic, AI-first partnership with Google Cloud will help the digital bank leverage the latter's "complete AI stack, from infrastructure, to platform, to models, to accelerate the development of innovative, consumer-centric products and creative campaigns".

The partnership is also expected to extend into security, with KLAR using Google Cloud's AI hardware to deploy graph neural networks to combat fraud and money laundering on its platform.

"Early pilots already show the potential: AI-driven creative concepts, from dynamic digital lookbooks to hyper-personalized product campaigns, boosted time spent in our app by 15% and increased orders by 50%. For us, this is proof that when creativity meets AI, the results can transform the way people shop," said Klarna's chief marketing officer, David Sandström.

On the other side, Affirm today announced its support for Google's Agent Payments Protocol, extending a long-standing partnership with the technology giant.

The AP2 is an open, payment-agnostic protocol developed with several companies to securely support agent-led payments across platforms.

Affirm said that by contributing to AP2, it is helping embed buy-now-pay-later directly into the architecture of agentic commerce.

data.title: Microsoft's emissions goals in question due to energy-hungry data centers: report
data.description:
Microsoft's (NASDAQ:MSFT) goal of reaching carbon-negative emissions by 2030 is in danger due to a combination of power-draining data centers and the limited supply of "clean electricity" in some East Asian nations, according to a report by Bloomberg.

Ken Haig, Microsoft's senior director of government affairs in Japan, said the company is competing "for a very small pie of available renewable power today" in Japan, South Korea and Taiwan, the report said. The struggle is also being felt by chip producers in the region. Emissions related to semiconductor production increased by 300% in 2024.

Despite big tech working for years to "decarbonize" operations, the rise of AI data centers and advanced chip demand has put pressure on limited renewable power sources, the report said. For example, Microsoft's "planet-warming impact" has increased by 23% over the past five years.

"We need our suppliers also to be getting onto 100% carbon-free energy," said Haig.

data.title: Institutional crypto exposure set to double by 2028, State Street survey finds
data.description:
With a friendlier regulatory environment under the Trump administration, nearly 60% of institutional investors plan to boost their crypto allocation in the coming year, and average exposure is expected to more than double within three years, according to State Street's annual digital assets and emerging technology survey released on Thursday.

The average portfolio allocation across a range of digital assets stood at 7%, the survey found, and that's expected to jump to 16% by 2028, undercoring growing confidence in the space as the U.S. clears the way for broader adoption with landmark policy moves, such as the passage of the stablecoin-focused GENIUS Act.

Among the most common vehicles used were digital cash and tokenized versions of public stocks or bonds, each making up about 1% of the average portfolio.

Private equity and private fixed income were expected to be the first asset classes to undergo tokenization, the process of turning real-world assets into digital tokens that can be traded on a blockchain. "This reflects a strategic focus among institutional investors on unlocking liquidity and efficiency in traditionally illiquid markets," the report said.

By 2030, a majority expect 10%–24% of institutional investments to be executed through tokenized instruments, which Robinhood Markets (HOOD) CEO Vlad Tenev expects "will east the entire financial system."

While stablecoins and tokenized assets make up most holdings, cryptos remain the main source of returns and are expected to stay that way over the near term. 

Some 27% of respondents said bitcoin (BTC-USD) generates the highest returns among their digital asset portfolios, while 25% predicted it will remain a top performer over the next three years. From there, 21% reported that ether (ETH-USD) was their current biggest returns generator, and 22% expect that to still be the case in three years.

Crypto prices were already hovering around all-time highs, with bitcoin (BTC-USD), up 31% year-to-date, changing hands at $122K at press time, and ether (ETH-USD), +30% YTD, trading $4.36K.

The State Street survey was conducted in partership with Oxford Economics, with responses from more than 400 institutional investors globally.

data.title: U.S. stocks decline as Powell passes on policy outlook commentary
data.description:
Wall Street's major indexes struggled to keep up momentum on Thursday, after the S&P 500 (SP500) and the Nasdaq (COMP:IND) hit new intraday highs, as Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell did not comment on the economic outlook or monetary policy at a Fed event.

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was last -0.2% in late morning trade, while the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was -0.2%, and the Dow (DJI) was -0.3%. 

Gold (XAUUSD:CUR) officially surpassed the $4,000 level on Wednesday, while the Silver spot silver also hit a historic $50/oz as investors piled into precious metals.

“Could this trigger a fresh wave of buying as retail investors rush to join the latest gold rush? It’s certainly possible, although such interest is likely to be in coins, jewelry and bullion. Nevertheless, every little helps. It looks as if gold may be consolidating after two months of relentless gains,” said David Morrison, senior market analyst at Trade Nation.

“All recent attempts at a retracement have been short-lived, with every dip quickly met by aggressive buying interest. This persistent demand underscores the depth of conviction among market participants, even at such high price levels,” he added.

In other news, Fed Chair Jerome Powell gave welcome remarks before the Community Bank Conference, however, he did not mention anything about the monetary policy or the economy. Fed Vice Chair for Supervision Michelle Bowman will also give opening remarks at the same event.

Also, due to the ongoing U.S. government shutdown, there are no economic data releases slated on Thursday.

“Last night saw the release of the minutes from the Federal Reserve’s September FOMC meeting… Despite this lack of clarity, the probability of two further rates cuts this year has risen, according to the CME’s FedWatch Tool, and this has helped to underpin U.S. equities. Overall, investors took the minutes in their stride, viewing them as neither overly dovish nor hawkish,” Morrison said.

Over in the bond market, yields were lower. The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was 3 basis points higher at 4.15%, while the 2-year yield (US2Y) also rose 2 basis points to 3.61%. Investors will await the results of a $22B, 30-Year Bond Auction.

The Fed balance sheet will land later in the day. 

On geopolitics, Israel and Hamas have agreed to a ceasefire and hostage deal as part of the first phase of a peace plan advanced by U.S. President Donald Trump to end the war in Gaza.

Wall Street’s major averages ended higher on Wednesday, with the Nasdaq Composite and S&P 500 tagging new record closes, all while gold (XAUUSD:CUR) topped $4,000/oz and FOMC minutes landed.

More on markets:

data.title: Biggest stock movers Thursday: TLRY, AKRO, DAL, RACE, and more
data.description:
Stock futures edged lower on Thursday as investors turned cautious after Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell refrained from commenting on the economic outlook or monetary policy during a Fed event, leaving markets awaiting further guidance on the central bank’s next move.

Here are some of Thursday's biggest stock movers:

Biggest stock gainers

  • Tilray Brands (NASDAQ:TLRY) +31% - Shares surged after the Canadian cannabis company posted its first revenue beat in four quarters and raised its full-year outlook. Q1 revenue rose ~5% Y/Y to $209.5M, topping estimates by ~$3.8M, driven by a 9% rise in distribution sales to $74M, while cannabis revenue grew ~5% to $64.5M and beverages were flat at $55.7M. Operating costs fell ~43% to $55.4M amid lower amortization and non-cash expenses, helping Tilray swing to $1.5M net income from a $34.7M loss a year ago. Adjusted EBITDA rose ~9% to $10.2M, and adjusted net income improved to $3.9M from a $6.1M loss, though gross margin slipped to 27% from 30%. The company now expects FY2026 adjusted EBITDA of $62M–$72M, above analysts’ $61.1M forecast.
  • Richardson Electronics (NASDAQ:RELL) +16% - Shares jumped higher after the company reported better-than-expected FQ1 results, driven by strong growth in its semiconductor wafer fab business. Excluding the divested Healthcare unit, net sales rose by 6.8% Y/Y. The company's focus on a more profitable sales mix and disciplined cost control was highly effective, leading its operating income to more than triple compared to the prior year. CEO Edward J. Richardson noted the solid results reflect the company's value proposition and market diversity, adding that they generated positive operating cash flow for the sixth consecutive quarter and are confident in delivering improved performance for the entirety of FY2026.
  • Akero Therapeutics (NASDAQ:AKRO) +16% - Shares advanced after the company announced a definitive agreement to be acquired by Novo Nordisk (NVO) for up to $5.2B in cash. Under the agreement, Akero shareholders will get $54.00 in cash for each share when the deal closes, plus a $6.00 payment that will be made if efruxifermin gets full U.S. regulatory approval for MASH-related compensated cirrhosis The upfront cash portion values Akero at $4.7B, representing a 42% premium to its May 19, 2025, closing price. The deal, unanimously approved by Akero’s board, is expected to close by year-end.
  • Delta Air Lines (NYSE:DAL) +6% - Shares rose after the carrier delivered a stronger-than-expected Q3, with non-GAAP EPS of $1.71, beating estimates by $0.19, and adjusted revenue of $15.2B, up 4.2% Y/Y and $120M above consensus. For FY2025, Delta guided EPS of around $6, exceeding the $5.77 consensus, with free cash flow of $3.5B–$4B and gross leverage below 2.5x. In Q4, the airline expects revenue growth of 2%–4% vs. the 1.24% forecast, an operating margin of 10.5%–12%, and EPS of $1.60–$1.90, with a midpoint of $1.75, above the $1.64 consensus.

Biggest stock losers

  • Ferrari (RACE) -13% - Shares slumped after the luxury automaker updated its long-term financial goals at its Capital Markets Day, outlining plans for steady growth and profitability through 2030. The company targets net revenue of around €9B by 2030, reflecting a ~5% CAGR, driven by high-value sports cars, customized models, and a robust order book. Profitability goals include at least €2.75B in EBIT with a 30% margin and €3.6B+ in EBITDA with a 40% margin, supported by premium and limited-edition products. Ferrari projects cumulative industrial free cash flow of about €8B over 2026–2030 after €4.7B in capex, while noting it has already surpassed its 2026 profit milestones. Between 2026 and 2030, Ferrari plans to launch an average of four new cars annually, with its 2030 lineup composed of 40% ICE, 40% hybrid, and 20% electric—lowering its EV target from 40%.
  • AZZ (NYSE:AZZ) -4% - Shares slid after the company missed FQ2 expectations and presented a mixed outlook for the full year. While AZZ maintained its FY2026 guidance, the midpoint for its projected sales of $1.675B came in slightly above the consensus of $1.66B. However, the midpoint for adjusted diluted EPS of $6.00 missed the analyst consensus of $6.04. The maintained guidance reflects expectations for anticipated market conditions, lower interest expense, and a 24% effective tax rate, while excluding any M&A activity or federal regulatory changes.

data.title: Lyft and Tensor Robocars to make consumer-owned AVs available on Lyft platform
data.description:
To keep up with competitors that are gaining ground in the robotaxi space, Lyft (NASDAQ:LYFT) is partnering with Tensor Auto to deploy consumer-owned robotaxis in Europe and North America beginning in 2027 on the Lyft (NASDAQ:LYFT) platform.  

Tensor Robocars will be available for private ownership, enabling owners to generate income through the Lyft (NASDAQ:LYFT) platform, in addition to providing an additional source of revenue for the ride-share company.

By introducing Tensor autonomous vehicles to its network and reserving hundreds for its own fleet, Lyft (NASDAQ:LYFT) is working to close the gap with rival Uber (UBER), which has accelerated its autonomous ambitions through partnerships with Waymo (GOOG), Momenta, WeRide (WRD), and Baidu (BIDU).

As the first company to develop a “Level 4 AV” designed for personal ownership, the Tensor Robocar provides an avenue to monetize robotaxis for individuals, representing an “important milestone in AV adoption.”

“Lyft has created opportunities for millions of people to earn on the platform, but right now, one of the last barriers to rideshare is time — you have to physically be in your vehicle to give rides and earn," said Jeremy Bird, Executive Vice President of Driver Experience at Lyft.

"Future owners will be able to start earning on the Lyft network the moment their vehicle rolls off the lot, while riders get access to some of the most advanced and luxurious AVs on the road. This is exactly the kind of innovation that expands what's possible in transportation."

Tensor Robocars feature L4 technology, powered by eight Nvidia (NVDA) DRIVE AGX Thor SoCs, with over 100 sensors within an agentic AI architecture that can process over 53 gigabits of sensor data per second.

Over the next few years, Tensor Robocars will be deployed across the U.S., Europe, and the Middle East to immediately onboard on Lyft’s (LYFT) platform and give owners instant access to the vehicle in markets where “Lyft-ready” L4 technology is available.

Lyft (LYFT) shares were initially nudged higher on the news before surrendering gains and retreating back into negative turf for a fifth straight day. 

Source: Press Release

data.title: LPL Financial raised to Market Perform at Raymond James
data.description:
Raymond James upgraded LPL Financial Holdings (NASDAQ:LPLA) to Market Perform from Underperform as analyst Wilma Burdis expects the fed funds target rate would have to fall "materially below 3% in 2026 to significantly pressure LPLA's intermediate-term earnings."

LPL's (NASDAQ:LPLA) Commonwealth acquisition is seen boosting earnings and diversifying away from client cash income; however, its organic net new asset growth may be more modest as it focuses on the Commonwealth acquisition, the analyst wrote in a note to clients.

Rich Steinmeier, who became CEO in October 2024, has increased focus on controlling general & administrative expenses, compared with his predecessor. "Core G&A is now guided to grow ~25% at the midpoint after the Commonwealth transaction for full-year 2025 year-over-year, while we estimate total advisory and brokerage assets are set to grow ~38%," Burdis wrote.

Raymond James lowered its Q3 2025 EPS estimate for LPLA to $4.40 from its prior estimate of $4.56. Its full-year 2025 EPS estimate dropped to $18.81 from $19.17, and its full-year 2026 EPS estimate was trimmed to $22.57 from $22.72.

LPL Financial (LPLA) stock rose 0.5% in Thursday morning trading.

The Market Perform rating sits between the SA Quant rating of Sell and the average Wall Street rating of Buy.
